blocks.text: DTC P2714: Lock-up clutch control SOL.V. OFF Stuck

The lock-up clutch control SOL.V. controls the hydraulic pressure applied to the lock-up clutch. The spool in the lock-up clutch control SOL.V. pushes a valve in response to the duty (load) output, which is converted to a current value by the PGM-FI ECU. As a result, the valve pushed by the spool releases oil pressure proportional to the current value. The PGM-FI ECU outputs a control signal for the lock-up clutch oil pressure for proper lock-up. If the lock-up clutch remains OFF for a predetermined period of time in the running state, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the lock-up clutch control SOL.V. is stuck OFF and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

items.text: Lock-up clutch control SOL.V. OFF sticking
Step on the brake pedal and move the select lever from the N to D position.
The vehicle speed [vehicle speed] is 60 km/h for 1 minute.
Set the select lever to the N position.
Repeat test run steps 2 through 5 five times.
Repeat test run steps 1 through 7 five times.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the D indicator light will flash without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the DTC and freeze data will be stored.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
